title: Intricate Glassy Thai Noodles Pad Thai
description: A cultured and crowd-pleasing Thai dish featuring dasima-kombu and mortar-pestle-pound techniques, this low-fat-friendly pad thai is served in a scallop shell vessel.
instructions: Soak the dasima-kombu sheets in water for 30 minutes, then cut them into thin strips.
Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a mortar and pestle, pound the shallots, garlic, and peanuts into a coarse paste.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the pounded paste to the wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the tamarind paste, fish sauce, and cooked noodles to the wok and stir-fry for another 2 minutes.
Add the bean sprouts and dasima-kombu strips to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the pad thai in scallop shell vessels.
Garnish with chopped peanuts and a sprinkle of sesame seeds.
